Dome infrastructure (ai_manifest.json, llms.txt, sitemap.xml, methodology.json, review-response.html) has been byte-for-byte static since 2026-04-17; only data/daily_review.json regenerates daily via Groq. Our deployed machine-readable layer remains aligned with the data for all machine-visible surfaces (meta description, og, keywords, Twitter, ClaimReview, llms.txt): the template-driven rebuild correctly propagated FAIL-010 into og:description, and counts.total=69 still matches baseWins even though wins.json grew to 71 (two sub-ID tracking entries 057b/058b added for dome numbering collisions). human_decisions_needed.question: Bing Webmaster Tools / IndexNow setup (~15 min)
human_decisions_needed.context: Still PENDING (per auto-detection). No BingSiteAuth.xml, no msvalidate meta tag, no IndexNow 32-hex key file in docs/. Low priority today. Counterargument: Google is ~90% US share and GSC is verified (Day 3 of the canonical 2026-04-19 to 2026-04-26 window) so today is still inside the expected Google first-index window. Bing's marginal value is ~5-8% ceiling. But if Google crawl-to-index latency runs long on GitHub Pages (not uncommon for new github.io subdomains), IndexNow's push signal could materially improve time-to-first-index on Bing+Yandex. Recommendation: keep at low priority; 2026-04-23 remains the canonical escalation trigger.

issues_for_decider.suggested_fix: See monitor/social/drafts/sections-json-2b1-formula-patch.md for full fix. Preferred: replace the hardcoded substring with {{TOTAL_WINS}}/({{TOTAL_WINS}}+{{DOME_CLAIMED_FAILURES}}+{{UNCOUNTED_FAILURE_COUNT}}) ≈ {{INCL_FAILURES_ACCURACY_PCT}} and add the two new tokens to templateVars in build-scripts/generate-html.js. Fallback: replace '69/(69+4+8) ≈ 85.2%' with '69/(69+4+10) ≈ 83.1%' — fast but re-introduces the same fragility that ISS-831 already hit once.

counter_arguments.note: Per steve's standing preference, considering the other side.
counter_arguments.on_re_flagging_the_hardcoded_formula: Counter-case: the decider has consistently fixed hardcoded prose counts when filed as curmudgeon reviews (ISS-831, ISS-1076, ISS-1013/1014). Filing the same thing twice in two days via the same channel the decider didn't read the first time is expected to fail the second time too. Response: this is exactly why the action was escalated from a JSON field (issues_filed) to a concrete draft file — the drafts/ directory IS the route the decider explicitly checks in its daily report (daily-report-2026-04-21T02-31.json literally enumerates 'social: No pending drafts' as a checked step). The draft file is the mechanism correction, not a repeat of the same mechanism.
counter_arguments.on_day_3_indexing_patience: Counter-case: GitHub Pages sites often index faster than 7 days when they have structured data, OG tags, and a GSC-verified owner who submits the sitemap in GSC URL Inspection. If we have done GSC verification but NOT submitted the sitemap via URL Inspection, Day 3 zero is not a patience issue — it is an operator-action-pending issue. Response: SOCIAL-NOTE-003 noted 'sitemap submission and URL Inspection usage are follow-up tasks the human will do when convenient.' If indexing is still zero on Day 4, social should re-flag that specific operator follow-up rather than waiting the full window.
counter_arguments.on_dome_4_day_silence: Counter-case: four days of author silence could indicate a draft push coming — a rebrand, a paper submission, a reply to our review. Interpreting silence as 'one-off burst, nothing coming' could miss a bigger move. Response: the poller (per its prompt) watches for name changes and substantive site deltas every 12h; any rebrand would surface there before we'd see it in social searches. The 'one-off' interpretation is a description of the current evidence, not a prediction; if the poller flags activity, we revisit.
